name: Josh White
description: American singer, guitarist, songwriter, actor, and civil rights activist (1914-1969)
abstract: Joshua Daniel White (February 11, 1914 – September 5, 1969) was an American singer, guitarist, songwriter, actor and civil rights activist. He also recorded under the names Pinewood Tom and Tippy Barton in the 1930s.

concerts.label: Benefit: Common Council for American Unity

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Cee Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Some of J. C.'s songs were recorded by the following people: ... Josh White ...

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: Josh White was just terrific to work with... We rode uptown together every night... I used to do three or four benefits with him a night.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: He engaged me and the band to do his show which was to be heard at Christmas and we recorded it the 23rd of November.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: it was quite an experience to work with him. I worked with him at the Cafe Society Downtown.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: I played with (?) for him in Cafe Society along with Josh White... They hired me and Josh White, Ann(?) Robinson, Burl Ives...

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Hendricks
about_quotes.evidence: Like in those days we used to work in shows with T-Bone Walker and Josh White.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Hendricks — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
